channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iran and US escalate Strait of Hormuz tensions amid military strikes
item.description: Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz indefinitely, accusing the US of continued interference in the region. The closure comes after Iranian forces struck two vessels in the strategic waterway, includi...

item.title: Censorship and Power: The Struggle of 'Satluj' Film
item.description: The article discusses the film 'Satluj,' which explores the dark realities of power and impunity in Punjab's past. Directed by Honey Trehan, the film follows Jaswant Singh Khalra, an activist who unco...

item.title: Canberra Raiders dominate New Zealand Warriors with historic 42-16 victory
item.description: The Canberra Raiders delivered a dominant performance against the New Zealand Warriors, securing a 42-16 win that sets a new NRLW record for the largest margin. The match, played at Canberra Stadium, ...

item.title: Pakistan and Iran Commit to Dialogue and Diplomacy for Regional Peace
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar and Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi emphasized dialogue and diplomacy as the sole solution to regional disputes during their phone conversation. Both leaders acknowl...

item.title: Extreme weather and tornadoes hit Saskatchewan and Manitoba as heat wave persists
item.description: A record-tying 10 tornadoes were reported across Saskatchewan on Friday as a dangerous heat wave intensified extreme weather across the Prairies. Environment Canada meteorologist Kayla Bilous confirme...

item.title: Horoscope Predictions for July 12, 2026: Insights for Each Zodiac Sign
item.description: This article provides personalized horoscope predictions for each zodiac sign on July 12, 2026. Aries is advised to balance duty with pleasure to avoid burnout, while Taurus must assert their boundari...

item.title: US Senator Lindsey Graham Dies at 71 After Sudden Health Emergency
item.description: US Senator Lindsey Graham, a prominent South Carolina politician, has died at the age of 71 following a 'brief and sudden illness.' The news was announced by his office on Saturday evening, with his f...

item.title: Vintage Advertising Campaigns: Lucinda Hollingsworth's 1960s Monet Jewelry Ads by Wingate Paine
item.description: This article explores the iconic advertising campaigns from the 1960s that featured model Lucinda Hollingsworth for Monet Jewelry, captured by renowned photographer Wingate Paine. The advertisements, ...

item.title: Kenyan-Born Teenager Signs Scholarship With Manchester United's Under-18 Team
item.description: Emmanuel Ziro, a 16-year-old Kenyan-born forward, has signed a scholarship with Manchester United's Under-18 academy, marking a significant milestone for Kenyan football. The young talent, who joined ...

item.title: Satellite Data Reveals Severe Flooding in Mindanao After Typhoon Inday
item.description: Satellite maps from the Philippine Space Agency, using Sentinel-1 data, show heavy flooding in Cotabato and Bangsamoro areas after Typhoon Inday (Bavi) hit Mindanao. The maps, captured on July 10, hig...

item.title: Africa's Renewable Energy Transition Focuses on Institutional Development
item.description: Africa's renewable energy transition is entering a new phase, shifting from proving clean energy's viability to establishing the institutional frameworks needed for large-scale deployment. Despite glo...

item.title: ICE Agent Shooting of Mexican Man Sparks Calls for Transparency and Accountability
item.description: A Mexican national, Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, was killed during an ICE-led traffic stop in Houston, Texas, according to investigative reports. Surveillance footage obtained by CNN shows his white van be...

item.title: Legendary playback singer S. Janaki passes away at 88, tributes pour in from across India
item.description: Veteran playback singer S. Janaki, who sang thousands of songs across South Indian films, passed away at the age of 88 in Mysuru on July 11, 2026. Her mortal remains were placed at Maharaja’s College ...

item.title: Pakistan Security Forces Eliminate Over 65 Terrorists in Operation Shaban
item.description: Pakistan's security forces have eliminated over 65 terrorists in the ongoing Operation Shaban, with the total number of militants killed since July 5 reaching 105. The joint operation involving the ar...

item.title: Tottenham Managers Clash Post-Sacking: A Battle for the Managerial Throne
item.description: The article discusses the intense rivalry between two former Tottenham Hotspur managers who have both been sacked by the club. Following their dismissals, the managers are now set to face off in a hig...

item.title: Johannesburg Faces Severe Cash Crisis and Infrastructure Challenges
item.description: The City of Johannesburg is grappling with a severe cash crisis, despite claims by Mayor Dada Morero that financial management is stable. The city is paying suppliers late, leading to suspended infras...

item.title: Kenya President Ruto's Inner Circle and 2027 Election Strategy
item.description: The article discusses Kenya President William Ruto's efforts to secure a second term in the 2027 general election. With a unified opposition coalition threatening his re-election, Ruto is relying on a...

item.title: Parliamentary committee revises anti-corruption bill clause on PM and CMs removal
item.description: A key parliamentary committee has acknowledged that the clause allowing removal of the Prime Minister, Chief Ministers, or ministers from office if they are in custody for 30 consecutive days is consi...

item.title: Phillip Island weather update and access troubleshooting
item.description: The article discusses the current weather conditions on Phillip Island, providing details about expected weather patterns. It also includes an error message from News Corp Australia regarding crawler ...

item.title: U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham Dies at 71 After Brief Illness
item.description: U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham (R-SC) died on Saturday, July 11, 2026, at the age of 71 from a 'brief and sudden illness,' according to his office. The statement noted that emergency responders were call...
